characters.physical_details: Bleach-rough hands, pinned hair, a chemical burn scar inside the right wrist, and a left knee stiff from years on metal stairs.

key_relationships.gabriel_anaya: mother of; estranged at story start
key_relationships.naila_quispe: detainee witness and ally in the public stoppage

characters.occupation_status: RailPassage route supervisor on Meridian West. Access downgraded in chapter 7, briefly restored under escort in chapter 14, suspended after chapter 16, then held for statement and compelled to testify.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes English and Spanish; handles rail schematics, compliance manuals, and chain-of-custody forms; knows fragments of Santa Avelinan dock slang.

state_transitions.change: Passcard access downgraded during Teresa's audit.
Becomes a fugitive employee while extracting Gabriel during blackout.
Returns to Caldera under escorted amber access.
Opens the greywater purge, is suspended, and enters municipal holding.
Testifies in the concession inquiry.

characters.availability: Present throughout; off-grid in chapter 13 and under legal supervision in chapters 17 and 18.

characters.physical_details: Lean and underfed, with an old scar through the left eyebrow, ink-stained fingers, and fresh bruising from intake restraint.

key_relationships.marisol_anaya: son of; estranged at story start
key_relationships.paloma_rivera: former child patient and later ally
key_relationships.ivo_dalmau: mother's old ally; knew him in childhood

characters.occupation_status: Before arrest, an oral-history collector and courier for Paper House. During the novel he is a detainee under false Santa Avelina paperwork, then a fugitive and public witness on provisional inland release.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes English and Spanish fluently; understands basic Santa Avelinan from recordings and inherited documents; hears accents and code words well.

state_transitions.change: Boarded under false Santa Avelina paperwork tied to Tom Avelar's certificate.
Moved into higher-security restraint tier after Teresa's audit.
Extracted in sanitation coveralls during the 21:17 blackout.
Smuggled back aboard in the hospitality load and hidden in a service cabinet.
Becomes a public witness by naming case 4471-R and the Santa Avelina assignment.
Placed on provisional inland witness release.
Transfer remains stayed; permanent legal status unresolved.

characters.availability: On page in chapters 1, 2, 4, 5, 8, and 10 through 18; indirect in 3, 6, 7, and 9.

characters.physical_details: Compact, silver-braided, tired-eyed, with a right-hand tremor after long shifts and clothes that smell of camphor, disinfectant, and basil.

key_relationships.gabriel_anaya: former child patient and later injured detainee
key_relationships.naila_quispe: patient and witness whose injuries force Paloma into action

characters.occupation_status: Trauma-room medic contracted to RailPassage. Visa and housing depend on line employment. Suspended at 14:10 in chapter 12 with seventy-two-hour housing protection, then held for immigration review after chapter 16.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes English and Spanish; fluent in medical shorthand, quarantine codes, and transport paperwork.

state_transitions.change: Backdates ketorolac treatment to ease Rufino's pain and protect delay.
Suspended after the false enteric-hold plan points security toward the trauma room.
Hands preserved samples and medical logs to municipal inspectors.
Held pending inquiry with immigration consequences tied to lost employment.

characters.physical_details: Tall, heavy-shouldered, grease under the nails, a small burn scar high on the neck, and a mechanic's gait.

key_relationships.teresa_ordonez: employer who treats him as an asset until he becomes a liability

characters.occupation_status: Senior mechanic at Caldera Junction, about nine weeks from pension vesting and carrying $6,800 in child-support arrears for daughter Lina. Hospitalized with an external fixator after chapter 16.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes English and Spanish competently, especially maintenance logs and schematics.

state_transitions.change: Creates a false waste-return fault to cover Marisol's deep clean.
Commits maintenance support to the public-stop plan.
Patches Gabriel's recorder into the hospitality audio path.
Suffers a crushed foot in the coupler gap while holding the sequence open.
Hospitalized under employer blame while insisting testimony include wages, arrears, housing, and pension pressure.

characters.availability: On page in chapters 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, and 14 through 17.

characters.physical_details: Immaculate uniforms, bitten cuticles, careful makeup over fatigue, and expensive reading glasses she keeps misplacing.

key_relationships.marisol_anaya: superior, former sponsor, and later opponent
key_relationships.gabriel_anaya: case subject whose file becomes bargaining leverage
key_relationships.paloma_rivera: medical contractor she disciplines through paperwork
key_relationships.ivo_dalmau: valuable mechanic until the sabotage turns him into risk
key_relationships.naila_quispe: detainee whose visibility threatens the concession narrative

characters.occupation_status: Regional compliance director for Meridian West. Uses audit authority, emergency variance, and private bargaining until municipal jurisdiction and the inquiry strip that control.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes legal and bureaucratic English and Spanish; fluent in treaty language, liability framing, and investor-facing compliance rhetoric.

state_transitions.change: Personally takes over the audit response.
Uses emergency variance to split suspect cars before outside health review.
Offers Gabriel's inland release as a private bargain.
Loses narrative control when municipal jurisdiction overrides containment.
Testifies in the concession review.

characters.availability: Central in chapters 4, 7, 12, 14, 15, 16, and 18; present by memo elsewhere.

characters.physical_details: High-altitude sun marks, cropped hair, two missing left-hand fingernails, and precise movement.

key_relationships.marisol_anaya: detainee witness who changes Marisol's risk threshold
key_relationships.gabriel_anaya: ally inside the car and organizing partner
key_relationships.paloma_rivera: injured patient and later medical witness
key_relationships.ivo_dalmau: brief ally through the stoppage plan

characters.occupation_status: Detainee from an inland drought-relocation zone and informal organizer. After the line halts she is placed in municipal shelter custody for testimony.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads Spanish well, reads English slowly, writes careful block letters, and uses tally marks and knots as memory aids.

state_transitions.change: Injured during the refusal crackdown and becomes a visible witness.
Identifies square-corner clinic holds and helps decode the rerouting scheme.
Leads detainees in throwing assigned-country tags onto the public platform.
Moved into municipal shelter custody as an inquiry witness.

characters.availability: Foreground in chapters 8, 10, 11, 12, 16, and 17; seen during the chapter 5 deep-clean displacement.

timeline.story_start: Early June 2043 at Puerta Seco during a heat surge, on the morning Gabriel is loaded toward the 7:40 departure.
timeline.estimated_span: Roughly four months, from early June intake to a late-September inquiry.

seasonal_track.season: Early summer, rising heat, long light, first storms not yet breaking.
High summer, oppressive heat, outages, storm buildups, and growing bodily exhaustion.
Late summer into early autumn, investor review season, municipal scrutiny, and first rain.

key_events.event: Marisol identifies Gabriel on intake and sees the Santa Avelina assignment under Tom Avelar's packet.
key_events.timing: Day 1, early June 2043; receiving state acceptance already stamped at 04:18.

key_events.event: Ancestral-admissibility rules make a pre-transfer appeal impossible.
key_events.timing: Within the first two days after intake.

key_events.event: A fabricated deep clean on Car 8 displaces thirty-one detainees and becomes Marisol's first sabotage.
key_events.timing: Early June, before the train clears the first major southbound segment.

key_events.event: Collective refusal breaks out during biometric rescan; Celia's wrist is broken and Naila is injured.
key_events.timing: Late June after mounting delays and tighter controls.

key_events.event: Marisol proves the clinic-hold and municipal-labor rerouting scheme and copies evidence out through Santa Marta before the 18:00 close.
Teresa uses emergency variance to split the consist; Julian Arce dies on Spur C at 11:37 and Paloma is suspended at 14:10.
key_events.timing: Early August during reconciliation week and extreme heat.

key_events.event: A 21:17 blackout lets Marisol extract Gabriel to Retiro Sur, where she confesses her earlier restraint-death cleanup.
key_events.timing: Mid-August during a regional outage and storm front.

key_events.event: RailPassage stages an investor-facing humane-mobility demo, and Gabriel's recorder is patched into the hospitality audio path.
key_events.timing: Late August, immediately before the concession review window.

key_events.event: Greywater, assigned-country tags, and live witness testimony spill into public view at Caldera; municipal inspectors seize the platform and Ivo's foot is crushed.
The late-September inquiry ends with Meridian West revoked pending interim public custody.

geography.primary_setting: Privatized Meridian West deportation corridor linking Puerta Seco intake, Caldera Junction, marshland layovers, and the southern approach to Puerto Ancla.

key_locations.name: Puerta Seco Intake Yard
key_locations.details: Inland dry-zone processing yard where detainees are scanned, sorted, cleaned, restrained, and loaded.

key_locations.name: Meridian West Rolling Stock
key_locations.details: The moving train: restraint cars, family bunks, trauma room, sanitation corridors, crew compartments, and blackwater systems.

key_locations.name: Caldera Junction
key_locations.details: Maintenance hub with crew dorms, cafeteria, pension office, reserve spur, commuter platform, and the public platform used in the rupture.

key_locations.name: Spur C Transfer Bridge
key_locations.details: Hot metal bridge between split consist sections at Caldera; Julian Arce dies here in chapter 12.

key_locations.name: Marsh Crescent Layover
key_locations.details: Tidal-flat stretch prone to outages, mosquitoes, humidity, and long stalled nights.

key_locations.name: Santa Marta Parish Archive
key_locations.details: Church archive under Caldera's commuter viaduct; Paper House routes copied records through it.

key_locations.name: Retiro Sur Rail Cemetery
key_locations.details: Weed-grown siding full of retired sleeper cars, stripped interiors, and rusting paint; hideout and confession site.

key_locations.name: Puerto Ancla Transfer Port
key_locations.details: Southern handoff point where rail custody meets ship, bus, or contracted receiving-state pickup.

key_locations.name: Old Freight-Claims Office
key_locations.details: City-retaken office facing the dead track at Caldera; converted into the late-September inquiry room.

distances: Puerta Seco is roughly one long operating day north of Caldera Junction when the corridor has priority.
Caldera Junction to Marsh Crescent is about six hours in clear conditions but much longer during holds or sanitation checks.
Puerto Ancla lies roughly two days south of Caldera by deportation-train timetable.
Spur C branches off Caldera's reserve stock and is used for split-consist transfers.
Santa Marta Parish Archive sits under Caldera's commuter viaduct and can be reached on foot from the hub.
Retiro Sur is close enough to Caldera to reach by service truck in under an hour.

world_rules: Under the Continental Mobility Compact, private concessionaires transport deportees across member-state territory using standardized custody categories and digital manifests.
Ancestral admissibility allows provisional receiving-state assignment through parentage, transit, or labor sponsorship; appeals usually wait until after transfer unless an outside court intervenes.
Quarterly reconciliation hardens provisional files, and quarter-close compression purges raw notes older than ten days unless tied to litigation.
Sanitation failures, unresolved biohazards, missing red-seal restraint inventories, and certain brake-test anomalies can legally halt a train.
A disputed-source emergency variance can split suspect cars before outside health review reaches them.
Medical-deferral categories can be rerouted into municipal stabilization or labor placements with reimbursements and transport-hold credits.
Housing deductions, some health coverage, and some visa protections depend on line employment, making refusal costly.
Once contamination hits a public platform, municipal health inspectors can take jurisdiction before RailPassage can recode it.

power_structure.over: Route workers, contracted medics, and the daily conditions of detainee transport
power_structure.mechanism: Wages, scheduling, housing deductions, contract discipline, and control of chain-of-custody records

power_structure.over: Marisol, Paloma, and the Meridian West audit response
power_structure.mechanism: Regional compliance authority, access permissions, disciplinary review, emergency variance, and investor-facing oversight

power_structure.over: Sanitation crews and route-level maintenance decisions
power_structure.mechanism: Route supervisor authority over blackwater readiness, trauma-room clearance, and dispatch-safe status
Restraint protocols, force authorization, and physical control of movement between cars

power_structure.over: People whose files have been assigned to their quotas or labor obligations
power_structure.mechanism: Treaty recognition, transfer acceptance, and ancestry or sponsorship claims

power_structure.holder: Municipal partner agencies buying deferred placements
power_structure.over: Medically deferred detainees rerouted into local work or stabilization contracts
power_structure.mechanism: Reimbursement agreements, intake quotas, and transport-hold credits

power_structure.over: RailPassage during public contamination or visible biohazard events
power_structure.mechanism: Emergency civic-health jurisdiction that can freeze a train in place

objects.name: Gabriel's audio recorder
objects.owner: Gabriel Anaya at story start, then Marisol, then the witness archive
objects.origin: Paper House field kit used to collect testimony from displaced and detained people
objects.status: Confiscated in chapter 2, stolen from property in chapter 3, patched into the hospitality demo in chapter 15, then entered as witness evidence.

objects.name: Notebook of names and bunk numbers
objects.owner: Gabriel Anaya, then shared with Marisol
objects.origin: Improvised record built from wrapper paper, bunk tags, and hidden exchanges inside the detainee car
objects.status: Begins as a hidden scrap in chapter 4 and survives the inquiry as part of the witness archive.

objects.name: Red-seal restraint key set
objects.owner: RailPassage route supervision, logged through Marisol's authority
objects.origin: Standard issue for emergency release and inventory checks on restraint hardware
objects.status: Mapped as a stoppage vulnerability in chapter 11 and still part of the inquiry record after chapter 16.

objects.name: Tom Avelar ancestry packet
objects.origin: Archived Santa Avelina dockworker certificate tied to Gabriel's dead father and used to justify ancestral admissibility
objects.status: Basis of Gabriel's false-country assignment until inquiry review.

objects.name: Paloma's pathology cooler
objects.origin: Trauma-room medical storage for preserved samples and temperature-sensitive evidence
objects.status: Used in the failed enteric-hold plan, then carried to municipal inspectors with preserved samples in chapter 16 and entered as city evidence.

objects.name: Marisol's supervisor passcard
objects.owner: Marisol Anaya until seizure after the public stop
objects.origin: RailPassage route-supervision credential tied to access across cars and yards
objects.status: Downgraded with an orange strip in chapter 7, partly restored under escort in chapter 14, and seized after chapter 16.

objects.name: Assigned-country tags and clipped-corner wristbands
objects.owner: RailPassage case-control system until detainees throw them into public view
objects.origin: Intake and rescan tagging used to sort families, transfer groups, and clinic holds
objects.status: Square-corner clinic-hold tags are decoded in chapter 10 and thrown onto the Caldera platform in chapter 16 as proof.

objects.name: Municipal reimbursement folder
objects.owner: Marisol Anaya, then Paper House and city investigators
objects.origin: Copied invoices including MW-CAL-77 and MW-MAR-12 showing municipal offsets on medically deferred detainees
objects.status: Leaked out through Santa Marta in chapter 10 and later supports the inquiry.

debts.amount: $14,200 remaining on a housing-and-emergency advance balance
debts.deadline: Collected through payroll until cleared; accelerated if employment ends
debts.status: Active through chapter 15 and destabilized by Marisol's suspension after chapter 16

debts.amount: $6,800 in child-support arrears owed by Ivo Dalmau
debts.status: Being reduced through overtime until his injury interrupts payment

income_sources.reliability: Stable until her suspension in chapter 16

income_sources.amount: Base pay plus heat-season overtime spikes
income_sources.reliability: Moderate and season-dependent before his injury

income_sources.amount: Fixed monthly payment with small shift bonuses
income_sources.reliability: Stable until her suspension in chapter 12 because work authorization is tied to the route

income_sources.source: RailPassage municipal offsets on medically deferred detainees
income_sources.amount: Invoices show $672 for four linen-recovery placements and $186 plus transport-hold credit for one drainage case
income_sources.reliability: Routine until chapter 10 leaks the scheme and chapter 16 exposes it

knowledge_state.knows: Gabriel is detainee case 4471-R and has been assigned to Santa Avelina through his dead father's ancestry packet.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: the crew and Teresa at first

knowledge_state.knows: Marisol recognized him immediately and is already bending procedure around him before she admits it.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Teresa, custody staff, and most detainees

knowledge_state.knows: Gabriel is Marisol's son and the first deep-clean delay is personal as well as procedural.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Teresa, audit staff, and most crew

knowledge_state.knows: Gabriel is Marisol's son and Marisol's delays are not mere clerical anomalies.
Square-corner tags mark clinic holds and can trace people rerouted out of the normal transfer stream.
RailPassage profits from medically deferred detainees through municipal labor contracts, and copied proof has already gone out through Santa Marta and Paper House.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: most crew, the public, and management's investor line

knowledge_state.knows: The stoppage plan only works if recorder audio, physical tags, and live witnesses reach a public platform before RailPassage can contain it.
Before the novel begins, Marisol once helped conceal a restraint death; chapter 13 confirms the details.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Paloma, Ivo, Teresa, and the inquiry until chapter 18

knowledge_state.knows: Gabriel's inland deferral, Marisol's housing debt, and Paloma's suspension can recover the recorder and leak chain before investor review.

environmental_constants: Bleach and ammonia never fully erase rust, old urine, wet restraint foam, and overheated metal.
The cars carry a floor vibration that changes pitch when couplings strain or blackwater shifts.
Summer heat keeps uniforms damp, tempers short, and plastic surfaces body-hot.
White paint on the deportation cars always fails at the seams, revealing rust beneath any claim of cleanliness.
